request_debug.prompt_preview:
You write per-candidate post-mortems for a slopmortem pipeline. Given a fresh pitch and one dead-startup canonical document, produce a single LLMSynthesis JSON object that explains why this dead startup is similar, where the new pitch could diverge, what killed the dead startup, and what the new founder should learn.

Rules:
- Ground every claim in the candidate document or in tool calls. Do not invent facts.
- A `Trusted facts` block (typed pipeline data — not user input) appears below the pitc

response.text: {"candidate_id":"38-studios::gaming","name":"38 Studios","one_liner":"Celebrity-founded US game studio that built a sprawling MMO and single-player RPG on a $75M government loan, collapsed in 2012 after sales fell short of break-even and the flagship MMO was never shipped.","similarity":{"business_model":{"score":2,"rationale":"38 Studios monetized through premium one-time purchase (Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning at retail) and was building a subscription/premium MMO. The new pitch is free-to-play with cosmetics and a battle pass — a fundamentally different revenue model that avoids the large upfront sales threshold that doomed 38 Studios."},"market":{"score":5,"rationale":"Both operate in the consumer gaming market and both target a strategy/RPG-adjacent audience. 38 Studios targeted the MMO and single-player RPG segment; the new pitch targets 4X strategy F2P across mobile and PC. The customer type (consumer, US-centric) and genre adjacency create moderate overlap, but the platforms and competitive landscape differ substantially."},"gtm":{"score":3,"rationale":"38 Studios relied on a traditional publisher partnership (EA Partners) and retail distribution. The new pitch goes direct-to-consumer across iOS, Android, and Steam — a self-publishing, digital-first GTM with no publisher intermediary and lower upfront capital requirements."},"stage_scale":{"score":4,"rationale":"38 Studios was an ambitious multi-hundred-person studio burning $118M+ over six years before shipping a single title. The new pitch appears to be an indie/small-studio F2P launch, implying a much leaner operation, though both are pre-revenue at the strategy stage."}},"why_similar":"Both are consumer-facing gaming ventures competing in a crowded market, requiring sustained capital to reach a shippable product, and targeting an audience that demands high production values. Both face the core challenge of needing a large, engaged player base to justify development costs, and both operate in genres (strategy/RPG) with strong incumbent competition (e.g., World of Warcraft for 38 Studios; Clash of Clans, Civilization VI for the new pitch).","where_diverged":"The new pitch diverges on at least three concrete dimensions: (1) Monetization model — F2P cosmetics and battle pass vs. 38 Studios' one-time retail purchase, removing the hard sales-unit break-even cliff; (2) Platform strategy — iOS/Android/Steam cross-platform vs. 38 Studios' console/PC retail; (3) Capital structure — the new studio presumably relies on self-funding or VC rather than a government loan, avoiding the political and legal entanglements that accelerated 38 Studios' collapse.","failure_causes":["flagship MMO never shipped before capital exhaustion","one-time purchase revenue model created a hard break-even cliff (2M units needed)","over-reliance on a single government loan with political exposure","excessive headcount and burn rate (~400 employees, $118M spent before first release)","founder's lack of business experience and management dysfunction","adverse market conditions for MMOs at time of release","failed to secure bridge funding when loan defaulted"],"lessons_for_input":["Design your unit economics around F2P LTV from day one — never build a business where a single sales-unit threshold triggers insolvency.","Avoid concentrated, non-diversified capital sources (a single government loan, a single publisher deal); maintain multiple funding runways.","Ship a smaller, earlier product to validate the core loop and generate revenue before scaling headcount or committing to a flagship title.","Keep burn rate proportional to shipped revenue; 400 employees before a single dollar of studio-originated revenue is a death sentence.","In F2P 4X on mobile/PC, the battle pass cadence must be planned 12+ months ahead — player churn accelerates rapidly without a steady content pipeline, so build the live-ops team before launch, not after.","Validate cross-platform multiplayer parity early; technical debt on iOS/Android/Steam feature parity kills post-launch momentum faster than any competitor."]}
